title: Add Multiple Whole Numbers
description: Add two or more whole numbers to find their total.
condition: Expression with two or more whole numbers connected by addition (e.g., a + b + c).
response: Compute the sum of the numbers.
label: Production / Skill

title: Add Quantities in Same Unit
description: After converting measurements to the same unit, add the quantities to find a total.
condition: Two measurements expressed in the same unit that must be combined into one total.
response: Add the numerical values and keep the common unit.
label: Principle / Model

title: Analyze Effect of Parentheses on Value
description: Compare two expressions by reasoning about how parentheses change grouping and therefore the size of the result, without fully evaluating.
condition: Two expressions differ only by grouping (parentheses) but contain the same numbers and operations.
response: Determine which expression is greater (<, >, =) by reasoning about how grouping changes the operations performed.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $108 - (81 + 3)$ and $108 - 81 + 3$. Since subtracting a larger grouped sum makes the result smaller, $108 - (81 + 3) < 108 - 81 + 3$.

title: Compare Place Value Magnitude
description: Determine multiplicative relationship between values of the same digit in different places.
condition: The same digit appears in two different place values within a number.
response: State how many times greater one value is than the other (e.g., divided by 10, multiplied by 100).
label: Principle / Model
example: In 668,284, the 6 in the hundred-thousands place is 100 times the 6 in the thousands place.

title: Compare Quotient to 10
description: Determine whether a quotient is less than 10 or greater than 10 based on estimation or division.
condition: A division expression and a prompt to classify the quotient as less than 10 or greater than 10.
response: State whether the quotient is < 10 or > 10.
label: Principle / Model
example: Estimate $311 \div 88 \approx 320 \div 80 = 4$, so the quotient is less than 10.

title: Compute Partial Product
description: Multiply two place-value components to find a partial product.
condition: Given two numbers or place-value parts to multiply (e.g., 30 and 600).
response: Compute their product using multiplication facts and place value.
label: Production / Skill

title: Compute Remainder in Division
description: After finding the greatest multiple of the divisor less than the dividend, compute the leftover amount.
condition: A division problem where the dividend is not an exact multiple of the divisor.
response: Subtract (divisor × quotient) from dividend to find remainder.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Metric Units Using Powers of 10
description: Convert between metric units by multiplying or dividing by powers of 10.
condition: A measurement in one metric unit (e.g., km, m, L, kg) must be expressed in a smaller or larger metric unit.
response: Multiply or divide by the appropriate power of 10 based on place value shifts.
label: Principle / Model

title: Divide by a Power of Ten
description: Divide a whole number by 10^n by shifting digits to the right n places.
condition: A whole number divided by 10^n.
response: Move the decimal point n places left (or remove n trailing zeros) to find the quotient.
label: Principle
example: $900{,}000 \div 10^3 = 900$ because the digits shift 3 places left.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Divide Multi-Digit Numbers
description: Compute quotient and remainder when dividing a multi-digit number by a one- or two-digit divisor.
condition: Expression of the form a ÷ b where a and b are whole numbers and b has one or two digits.
response: Use long division or partial quotients to find the whole-number quotient and remainder.
label: Production / Skill

title: Divide Whole Numbers
description: Divide one whole number by another.
condition: Expression of the form a ÷ b.
response: Compute the quotient (and remainder if needed).
label: Production / Skill

title: Estimate Product by Rounding
description: Round factors to compatible numbers to produce a reasonable multiplication estimate.
condition: Given a word problem requiring a reasonable estimate of a product (e.g., 292 × 2,483).
response: Round each factor to a nearby compatible number and multiply (e.g., 300 × 2,500).
label: Principle
notes: Rounding choice may vary slightly (???). [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Evaluate Power of 10
description: Interpret 10 raised to a whole-number exponent as repeated multiplication of 10.
condition: An expression of the form 10^n where n is a whole number.
response: Rewrite as 1 followed by n zeros.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Digit Place Value
description: Determine the value of a digit based on its position in a multi-digit number.
condition: A specific digit in a given place within a base-10 number.
response: State the digit’s value (e.g., 6 thousands = 6,000).
label: Principle / Model
example: In 912,084, the 2 is in the thousands place, so its value is 2,000.

title: Insert Parentheses to Make Equation True
description: Determine where to place parentheses in an expression so that the equation evaluates to a given value.
condition: An equation with missing parentheses and a specified result.
response: Insert parentheses to group operations so the equation becomes true.
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Division as Number of Groups
description: Use division to determine how many equal groups of a given size can be made from a total.
condition: Word problem asking how many groups of size b can be made from total a.
response: Set up and compute a ÷ b.
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Quotient and Remainder in Context
description: Explain the meaning of the quotient and remainder from a division expression within a word problem context.
condition: A division expression is given and a contextual explanation of quotient and remainder is requested.
response: State what the quotient represents in the situation and explain what the remainder means in context.
label: Rule / Plan
example: For $5{,}050 \div 75$, the quotient represents the number of full groups of 75, and any remainder represents the amount left over that does not make another full group.

title: Interpret Remainder in Context
description: Explain the meaning of the quotient and remainder in a real-world division situation.
condition: Given a division situation with context and a quotient with possible remainder.
response: State what the quotient represents and explain what the remainder means in the context (e.g., leftover items or need for an extra group).
label: Principle
example: For $4{,}724\div85$, the quotient is the number of full boxes; the remainder is DVDs that do not fill a complete box.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Multiply Multi-Digit Numbers
description: Multiply whole numbers with two or more digits using standard or distributive strategies.
condition: Expression of the form a × b where a and b are multi-digit whole numbers.
response: Compute the product using an algorithm or decomposition.
label: Production / Skill

title: Multiply or Divide by a Power of 10
description: Compute products or quotients involving whole numbers and powers of 10 by shifting place value.
condition: A whole number multiplied or divided by 10^n.
response: Shift digits left (multiply) or right (divide) by n places.
label: Principle / Model

title: Relate Standard, Expanded, and Exponential Forms
description: Match a power of ten written in standard form, repeated multiplication, and exponential notation.
condition: Given one form of a power of ten (standard, expanded multiplication, or exponential).
response: Write an equivalent representation in the other forms.
label: Principle

title: Represent Division with Area Model
description: Use an area (rectangular) model to represent division and identify partial quotients.
condition: Given a division expression and asked to model it with a rectangle showing partial quotients.
response: Partition the rectangle into sections whose areas are multiples of the divisor and label partial quotients that sum to the total quotient.
label: Production / Skill
example: For $6{,}384 \div 57$, split into $57 \times 100 = 5{,}700$, $57 \times 10 = 570$, and $57 \times 2 = 114$, giving partial quotients $100 + 10 + 2 = 112$.

title: Solve Multi-Step Multiplicative Word Problem
description: Model and solve a word problem involving multiplication and division to find number of groups.
condition: Given a context with total items formed by multiplication and a per-group usage rate, asked for number of days or groups.
response: Multiply to find total quantity, then divide by rate to find number of groups or days.
label: Principle

title: Subtract Whole Numbers
description: Subtract one whole number from another.
condition: Expression of the form a − b.
response: Compute the difference.
label: Production / Skill

title: Translate Equal Groups Situation to Division
description: Model a real-world equal groups situation using division to determine number of groups.
condition: A word problem describes a total amount and a constant amount per group.
response: Set up and compute a division expression (total ÷ amount per group) to find the number of groups.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 95 bottles hold 16 ounces each, total ounces $= 95 \times 16$. Days of water $= (95 \times 16) \div 76$.

title: Translate Multiplicative Word Problem
description: Represent a real-world multiplicative situation with an equation.
condition: Given a context describing equal groups (e.g., 292 rooms at $2,483 each).
response: Write a multiplication equation representing total cost (e.g., $292 \times 2{,}483$).
label: Principle
notes: Supports both estimation and exact computation.

title: Translate Product Phrase
description: Translate verbal phrases indicating multiplication into symbolic expressions.
condition: Phrase such as 'product of a and b' or 'multiply a by b.'
response: Write a × b.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Translate Quotient Phrase
description: Translate verbal phrases indicating division into symbolic expressions.
condition: Phrase such as 'quotient of a and b' or 'divide a by b.'
response: Write a ÷ b.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Translate Sum or Difference Phrase
description: Translate verbal phrases indicating addition or subtraction into symbolic expressions with appropriate grouping.
condition: Phrase such as 'sum of a and b' or 'difference of a and b.'
response: Write (a + b) or (a − b), using parentheses when needed.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Use Parentheses to Control Order
description: Insert or interpret parentheses to ensure operations are performed in the intended order.
condition: Given an expression with multiple operations and asked to make it true by inserting parentheses or to interpret grouping.
response: Place or use parentheses so grouped operations are evaluated first according to order of operations.
label: Principle
example: $60\times(50+2)+26=3{,}146$ because the sum is evaluated before multiplication.

title: Write Grouped Expression from Context
description: Represent repeated groups or combined quantities using parentheses and multiplication.
condition: Word problem describing a total per group multiplied by number of groups, or sum multiplied by a factor.
response: Write expression of the form (a + b + …) × n or similar grouped structure.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Connects structure of situation to symbolic grouping.
